key_departures.impact: 9-year Brown released with post-June 1 designation. Was the primary receiving TE alongside Harold Fannin Jr. in 2025. His departure removes a veteran receiving/blocking presence; Fannin Jr. now must anchor the position alone alongside depth pieces.

7-year Brown released with post-June 1 designation after being benched mid-season in 2025. Was a former All-Pro right guard. His departure, combined with Joel Bitonio's expected retirement, leaves a massive interior line void.

7-time Pro Bowl left guard's contract voided. Spent all 12 NFL seasons with Cleveland. Effectively departed after the Browns proceeded with Zion Johnson signing without waiting for his decision, making it unlikely he returns. Leaves a massive void at LG.
Released outright (not post-June 1). Injury-plagued RT who started only 8 games in 2025. Departure was telegraphed by Tytus Howard trade. Creates no new need since Howard replaces him.
Mid-season trade acquisition who started 12 of 13 games at LT in 2025. His departure as a UFA leaves left tackle as a significant unresolved need heading into the draft.

Starting center who tore his Achilles in Week 14 of 2025. Contract voided. Part of the complete offensive line overhaul. Center remains an unaddressed need after Elgton Jenkins signing partially covers it.

Posted career highs of 125 tackles and 3 INTs in 2025. Signed 3yr/$30M ($21M gtd) with Bears after Browns passed at $10M/yr. Significant departure of the defensive starter who was the lone impending free agent starter on defense.
Rotational DE who logged 310 snaps in 2025 with 2.5 sacks. Signed 1yr/$3.1M with Atlanta. Loss reduces EDGE depth but does not create a critical starter-level need.

Buried on depth chart behind Quinshon Judkins and Dylan Sampson in 2025 with only 176 total yards. Signed 1yr deal with Washington. Minimal impact since Judkins/Sampson are locked in as the 1-2 punch.

contract.notes: Existing 2026 contract year + new 3yr/$63M extension; trade cost was a 2026 5th-round pick (originally from Raiders)

signings.analysis: Howard brings versatility — he has played RT, RG, and LG — and is the most significant OL acquisition. The 3yr/$63M extension with $43M guaranteed signals real commitment. He is expected to start at RT, replacing Jack Conklin. However, LT remains completely unaddressed: Cam Robinson is gone, and Dawand Jones (rehabbing a serious leg injury) is the only internal option. OT need is partially addressed on the right side but remains critical on the left.

Top guard on the market, 26 years old, started 65 of 66 career games. 3yr/$49.5M with $32.4M guaranteed is a genuine investment at IOL starter-tier. Ranked 2nd in run block win rate (79.3%) in 2025 — ideal for Todd Monken's run-first philosophy. Addresses one of the two interior starter holes (LG/RG) left by Teller and Bitonio departing. However, center remains unaddressed after Pocic's exit, and a second interior starter spot (contingent on Bitonio retirement) still needs resolution. Need partially addressed — significant upgrade at guard but center void remains.

Two-time Pro Bowl versatile lineman who can play guard or center — directly addresses both the guard and center voids. Released by Packers (not UFA), so no comp pick implications. 2yr/$24M with $20M guaranteed is a strong commitment. His pass block win rate in limited 2025 action was elite (94.4%, would have ranked 17th). Combined with Zion Johnson, the two signings go a long way toward solving interior line. Jenkins likely slots at C or RG, making him the center answer. IOL need is now meaningfully reduced though not fully solved.

Re-signed 2021 second-round pick who played 17 games with 4 starts in 2025. One-year prove-it deal. Could compete to start at RG if the Johnson/Elgton Jenkins alignment puts him in competition. At $4M on a 1-year deal, this is a depth/swing signing, not a solution. Adds roster flexibility and interior competition but doesn't move the needle on the IOL need on its own.

contract.notes: Reported as 'up to $17M'; exact guaranteed figure not yet disclosed. Estimated $8-10M in guarantees based on structure.

signings.analysis: Former first-team All-Pro (2023) who had a down 2025 (83 tackles in 13 games) after being denied extension by Jets. Reunited with new Browns DC Mike Rutenberg, who was his LB coach for four seasons in New York. 2yr/$17M is mid-market starter money at LB ($8-16M AAV range). Williams replaces Bush at an $8.5M AAV vs Bush's $10M. Partially addresses the LB hole — he's a proven starting-caliber player with scheme familiarity, but at age 29/30 entering a bounce-back year, this is a bridge starter, not a long-term solution. LB drops from a top-tier need but remains a position to address in the draft.

2022 sixth-round pick who started 17 games for San Francisco in 2025 with a 37% run stop win rate — 19th among all DL, one spot behind Browns DT Mason Graham. 1-year/$3M is a depth/rotational signing. Adds interior line depth behind Mason Graham and Maliek Collins, compensating for Cameron Thomas's departure and Shelby Harris's departure. Minimal impact on the IDL need picture — depth only.

contract.notes: 1-year deal; financial terms not disclosed as of this update

signings.analysis: Primarily a blocking TE — 81% of his snaps have come from an inline alignment. Has not exceeded 123 receiving yards in a season. He was signed to complement Harold Fannin Jr., not replace David Njoku as a receiving option. Todd Monken's two-TE sets will use Stoll as the blocking piece and Fannin as the receiver. This does not solve the receiving TE depth hole left by Njoku — TE remains a need for a receiving-capable backup.

Tendered as RFA after starting all 17 games in 2025 with 103 tackles, 7 PDs, and 2 INTs. The right-of-first-refusal tender at $3.5M locks him in barring a matching situation. S was not a critical need, and retaining Hickman ensures it doesn't become one. Safety is stabilized.

contract.notes: Re-signed; financial terms not yet disclosed

signings.analysis: Depth CB who appeared in 10 games in 2025. Re-signing keeps the CB room intact at the depth level. Does not impact the starter picture — Denzel Ward and Tyson Campbell are the starters. Minimal need impact.

contract.notes: Re-signed; financial terms not yet disclosed

signings.analysis: Signed off the Texans' practice squad mid-season in 2025, played 7 games. Re-signing is a depth/camp competition move. No impact on starter-level CB needs.

contract.notes: Re-signed; financial terms not yet disclosed

signings.analysis: Holds Browns single-season records for punt average (49.4 in 2023), net punt average (42.5 in 2023), and punts inside the 20 (36 in 2024). Re-signing secures one of the top punters in football. No draft need created.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Tytus Howard (3yr/$63M, $43M gtd) solves RT. But LT is fully open after Cam Robinson departed and Jack Conklin was cut. Dawand Jones is the only internal LT option and is rehabbing a serious injury. LT is arguably the top remaining need on the roster and a first-round draft target. Drops from critical (both sides) to high-need (one side, LT specifically).

Zion Johnson (3yr/$49.5M) + Elgton Jenkins (2yr/$24M) + Teven Jenkins re-sign collectively address all three interior spots. Johnson covers LG/RG, Elgton Jenkins covers C/G swing, and Teven Jenkins provides depth. IOL was the most catastrophic pre-FA need (all 5 OL starters leaving) and is now largely resolved. Center is the last real question mark — Elgton Jenkins is the likely answer there — but this moves from tier 1 emergency to tier 3 depth need. A developmental IOL in mid-rounds still makes sense.

Quincy Williams (2yr/up to $17M) is a legitimate starting LB replacement for Devin Bush. Reunited with DC Rutenberg who knows how to deploy him. However, Williams is 29 turning 30, had a down 2025 season, and is on a short-term deal. The team still needs a long-term LB answer — Carson Schwesinger is locked in at one spot, but Williams is a bridge at the other. A day 2 LB prospect in the draft remains advisable. Need reduces from tier 2 to tier 3.

David Njoku's departure was a real loss of a veteran receiving TE. Jack Stoll (1yr, blocking specialist) is not a receiving replacement — he was explicitly signed to complement Harold Fannin Jr. in two-TE sets. Fannin Jr. is a legitimate weapon and can anchor the position offensively. Stoll handles the blocking role. The need for a receiving-capable TE2 remains, but Fannin Jr.'s emergence makes this tier 3 rather than tier 2. Draft a TE in rounds 3-5.

Mason Graham is locked in as the anchor. Kalia Davis (1yr/$3M) adds rotational depth. Maliek Collins is on roster. Cameron Thomas's loss and Shelby Harris's departure remove depth, and Davis partially replaces Thomas's rotational role. IDL need unchanged at tier 3 — depth/competition signing didn't escalate or fully resolve the position.

Zero WR signings of note. The Browns' WR room was their most criticized unit in 2025. No external additions have been made. DeAndre Carter (depth) departed. Need is unchanged — WR remains a high-priority draft position, likely round 1 or 2.
Ronnie Hickman tendered as RFA after a full 17-game starting season. Safety is stabilized. Can drop to tier 4 — developmental pick only if a value presents itself.

Martin Emerson Jr. departed (Achilles rehab, terms unknown as of update — listed as re-signed on some trackers but no confirmed deal). Ward and Tyson Campbell are the starters. Avery and Ross re-signed for depth. CB depth is thin behind the top two, but starters are set. Unchanged at tier 3.

No QB additions. Browns have not addressed QB in free agency — other teams added veterans (Murray to Vikings, Geno Smith to Jets, Willis to Dolphins) but Cleveland stands pat with Shedeur Sanders (likely 2026 draft pick) and Deshaun Watson (restructured). QB remains the highest-stakes roster question heading into the draft. Tier 1 unchanged.

key_additions.headline: Versatile veteran tackle (RT/RG/LG) anchors massive OL overhaul on a long-term deal
Top guard on market (2nd in run block win rate in 2025) signed to replace departed Teller and Bitonio
Two-time Pro Bowl C/G adds elite pass-blocking versatility and likely solves center void
Former first-team All-Pro LB reunites with DC Rutenberg to replace Devin Bush next to Schwesinger
Rotational DT (37% run stop win rate in 2025) adds interior depth behind Graham and Collins
Blocking TE specialist complements Harold Fannin Jr. in Monken's two-TE sets
Re-signed interior depth/swing guard adds competition and OL insurance
Full-time starter (17 games, 103 tackles) retained via RFA tender to anchor the safety spot

key_losses.headline: 7-time Pro Bowler's 12-year Cleveland tenure ends; Zion Johnson signed as replacement
9-year Brown's release closes a chapter; Harold Fannin Jr. now the clear TE1 anchor
Former All-Pro RG departs after being benched mid-2025; Elgton Jenkins signing is the replacement
Career-year LB (125 tackles, 3 INTs) departs; Browns pivot to Quincy Williams at lower AAV
Starting LT who joined mid-2025 departs, leaving LT as the top remaining unaddressed need
Injury-plagued RT released; Howard trade directly replaces his role on the right side
Rotational DE (2.5 sacks) departs; Kalia Davis signing partially compensates for DL depth loss
